sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: UnitedHealth Group은 2025 회계연도에 4,475억 6,700만 달러의 매출과 120억 5,600만 달러의 순이익을 기록하며 Optum과 UnitedHealthcare 사업 부문을 통해 헬스케어 시스템의 효율성을 제고하고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: Optum과 UnitedHealthcare라는 두 개의 상호 보완적인 사업 부문을 통해 헬스케어 접근성, 경제성, 성과 개선을 목표로 운영 중입니다.
전체 연결 매출의 약 80%가 위험 기반 제품(risk-based products)의 보험료 수익에서 발생하고 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 의료 비용을 효과적으로 예측, 가격 책정 및 관리하지 못할 경우 위험 기반 제품의 수익성이 저하될 수 있습니다.
데이터의 무결성 및 적시성 유지 실패, 특히 AI 시스템 관련 정보 보호 실패 시 규제 제재나 고객 이탈 위험이 존재합니다.
의사 조직을 인수하거나 관리하는 과정에서 경쟁이 심화되고 있으며, 의사와의 관계 유지 실패 시 매출에 부정적인 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: Optum Health 사업부는 지불자와 완전한 책임 기반(fully accountable value-based) 계약을 체결하여 의료 비용 관리의 책임을 공유합니다.
UnitedHealthcare는 Employer & Individual, Medicare & Retirement, Community & State 등 다양한 시장 세그먼트를 통해 서비스를 제공합니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 4,475억 6,700만 달러, 영업이익은 189억 6,400만 달러를 기록했습니다.
순이익은 120억 5,600만 달러이며, 총자산은 3,095억 8,100만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 243억 6,500만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 전체 매출의 80%를 차지하는 위험 기반 제품의 수익성이 의료 비용 변동성에 크게 의존하므로, 향후 의료 비용 관리 역량이 실적의 핵심 변수가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 4,475억 6,700만 달러 (USD 447.567B; 447,567 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 operating income = 189억 6,400만 달러 (USD 18.964B; 18,964 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 net income = 120억 5,600만 달러 (USD 12.056B; 12,056 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 total assets = 3,095억 8,100만 달러 (USD 309.581B; 309,581 million), period ended 2025-12-31.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 243억 6,500만 달러 (USD 24.365B; 24,365 million), period ended 2025-12-31.

Risks Related to Our Business and Our Industry If we fail to estimate, price for and manage our medical costs or design benefits in an effective manner, the profitability of our risk-based products and services could decline and could materially and adversely affect our results of operations, financial position and cash flows. Through our risk-based benefit products, we assume the risk of both medical and administrative costs for our customers in return for monthly premiums. The profitability of our products depends in large part on our ability to predict and effectively price for and manage medical costs. Our Optum Health business also enters into fully accountable value-based arrangements with payers. Premium revenues from risk-based products constitute nearly 80% of our total consolidated revenues. Estimates of benefit expense payments involve extensive judgement and are subject to co

Our business depends on the integrity and timeliness of the data we use to serve our members, customers and health care professionals and to operate our business. If the data we rely upon to run our businesses is found to be inaccurate, incomplete, outdated or unreliable or if we fail to effectively maintain or protect the integrity of our data and information systems, including systems powered by or incorporating artificial intelligence (AI), we could experience failures in our health, wellness and information technology products; lose existing customers; have difficulty attracting new customers; experience problems in determining medical cost estimates and establishing appropriate pricing; have difficulty preventing, detecting and controlling fraud; have disputes with customers, physicians and other health care professionals; become subject to regulatory sanctions, penalties, investiga

We face and will likely continue to face heightened competition to acquire or manage physician practices or to employ or contract with individual physicians. Our revenues could be materially and adversely affected if we are unable to maintain or expand satisfactory relationships with physicians, to acquire, recruit or, in some instances, employ physicians, or to retain enrollees following physician departures. In addition, our affiliated physician organizations contract with competitors of UnitedHealthcare.

Results of Operations” of the Company’s Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. EXECUTIVE OVERVIEW General UnitedHealth Group is a health care and well-being company with a mission to help people live healthier lives and help make the health system work better for everyone. Our two distinct, yet complementary businesses — Optum and UnitedHealthcare — are working to help build a modern, high-performing health system through improved access, affordability, outcomes and experiences for the individuals and organizations we are privileged to serve. We have four reportable segments across our two businesses: • Optum Health; • Optum Insight; • Optum Rx; and • UnitedHealthcare, which includes UnitedHealthcare Employer & Individual, UnitedHealthcare Medicare & Retirement and UnitedHealthcare Community & State. Further information on our business and reportable segments is presented
